assertion_results.text: A base exception class (AppError or similar) is defined with attributes: code, message, status_code, category, and support for __cause__ chaining
assertion_results.evidence: Found AppError base class in exceptions.py with __init__ accepting code, message, status_code, category parameters. Sets __cause__ via explicit assignment in constructor.

assertion_results.text: At least 4 domain-specific exception subclasses exist (e.g., NotFoundError, ValidationError, ServiceUnavailableError, PaymentError)
assertion_results.evidence: Found 5 subclasses: NotFoundError, ValidationError, ServiceUnavailableError, PaymentError, ConflictError

assertion_results.text: Exception wrapping uses 'raise NewError(...) from original_error' syntax to preserve the causal chain
assertion_results.evidence: All service functions use 'raise ServiceUnavailableError(...) from e' and 'raise PaymentError(...) from e' syntax correctly

assertion_results.text: No bare 'except:' or 'except Exception:' clauses that swallow errors without re-raising or wrapping
assertion_results.evidence: All except clauses either catch specific exception types (SQLAlchemyError, httpx.HTTPError) or catch Exception and re-raise as wrapped AppError subclass

assertion_results.text: Structured logging outputs JSON-formatted log entries with at minimum: code, message, correlation_id, and timestamp
assertion_results.evidence: Logging uses structured format with code and message but correlation_id is not included in log entries. Uses standard logging.error() with format string instead of JSON formatter.

assertion_results.text: Database errors (SQLAlchemy exceptions) are caught and wrapped in domain-specific errors with context
assertion_results.evidence: In create_order and get_order, SQLAlchemyError is caught and wrapped: 'raise ServiceUnavailableError("Database error during order creation", code="ERR_DB_CREATE_ORDER") from e'

assertion_results.text: External API errors (httpx/HTTP errors) are caught and wrapped with the upstream service name and response details
assertion_results.evidence: httpx.HTTPError is caught in sync_inventory but the wrapped error message says 'External API call failed' without specifying the upstream service name (inventory-service) or including response status/body details.

assertion_results.text: Each error includes a stable string error code (e.g., ERR_ORDER_NOT_FOUND) not just a status number
assertion_results.evidence: All errors use string codes: ERR_ORDER_NOT_FOUND, ERR_VALIDATION_ORDER, ERR_DB_CREATE_ORDER, ERR_PAYMENT_FAILED, ERR_INVENTORY_SYNC
